title: Exporting Time Tracking To Excel Questions
excerpt: So we have a sort of time sheet set up for each person broken down by client then by task. Our CFO would like to be able to export from this board by a specific time period. For example, he’ll need to look at June 1 - Ju…

posts.author: MStevens (Melissa Stevens)
posts.cookedHtml:
So we have a sort of time sheet set up for each person broken down by client then by task. Our CFO would like to be able to export from this board by a specific time period. For example, he’ll need to look at June 1 - June 15th and say Melissa spent 40 hours on X client, 25 on y client and 15 on Z client over that 2 week period. The export file doesn’t make it easy to see the associated dates. You can see on the 2nd tab of the export that there are dates but you don’t see the Customer that the task was associated with.

Is there an option I’m missing when we export the time tracking columns to excel?

posts.author: Tom-monday.com (Tom Ronen)
posts.cookedHtml: Have you tried filtering the board by person/client and then exporting to Excel?

posts.author: MStevens (Melissa Stevens)
posts.cookedHtml: The boards are specific to each person and have groups for each client. What I need to be able to do is export by date just like the Dashboard View options.

posts.author: straister (Shelly Traister)
posts.cookedHtml: @MStevens did you ever find an answer to this? I’ve had to workaround in excel macros

posts.author: ebannister (Elliot Bannister)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m in a similar situation. We use the Time Tracking column to record how long an assignee spends on an item, and they’re paid accordingly. We need to be able to see how much time each Person has spent on each task over the course of the month (the Time Tracking cell sometimes has logs from a few different months but as far as I know there’s no way to filter that).

I have played around with the Time Tracking dashboard, but unless I’m mistaken, there’s not a way to export or even print a list of tasks (with time spent) that has been filtered by Person and month.

posts.author: MStevens (Melissa Stevens)
posts.cookedHtml: Sort of. We have to literally add a new 2 week time tracking column to everyone’s timesheet each period so our CFO can export.
